title: Duplicated imports, DATABASE constant, and Flask app in app.py
evidence: app.py:1-15 declares `import`s, `DATABASE`, and `app = Flask(__name__)` twice; the second `DATABASE = 'books.db'` (relative) at app.py:14 overrides the absolute-path one at app.py:7
suggestion: Delete the duplicated lines 9-15; keep a single import block and one DATABASE definition (prefer the absolute path so the DB location is cwd-independent)

title: simple_test.py is a live-server integration script collected as a test
evidence: simple_test.py:11-95 spawns `app.py` as a subprocess on port 5001 and calls it via `requests`; the coverage scorer measured only this file (.coverage records simple_test.py with 1 covered line), yielding test_coverage=0.19 despite the 12-test tests.py suite
suggestion: Rename to a non-test name (e.g. smoke_check.py) or guard collection so the real unittest suite in tests.py drives the coverage metric; avoids requiring a free port and the `requests` dependency during scoring

title: No dependency manifest and no `pip install flask` step
evidence: no requirements.txt or pyproject.toml in run_dir; README.md:29-34 Setup omits installing Flask (only `python app.py`); simple_test.py also needs `requests`
suggestion: Add requirements.txt (flask, requests) and a `pip install -r requirements.txt` line to README setup

title: ?author= filter uses LIKE substring match rather than exact match
evidence: app.py:97 `SELECT * FROM books WHERE author LIKE ?` with `%author%`
suggestion: Acceptable per spec (filter exists); note it matches substrings, which may be intentional but is broader than an exact-author filter

title: Reported test_coverage is 0.19 (measured against simple_test.py, not tests.py)
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.19, defect_rate=1.0; .coverage measured simple_test.py only. R12 still satisfied: 12 tests exist and coverage>0
suggestion: Harness note: point the coverage scorer at tests.py so the number reflects the real unittest suite
